title: selaginella rupestris subsp. recurva
intro: Selaginella rupestris subsp. recurva, also known as the Rock Spike Moss or Rock Spikemoss, is a species of evergreen, perennial plant belonging to the Selaginellaceae family, commonly found in tropical and subtropical regions. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly its antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which have been shown to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and enhancing immune function. In herbal preparations, Selaginella rupestris subsp. recurva is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to support overall health and well-being. However, further research is needed to confirm the effectiveness and safety of using this plant for medicinal purposes, and it should be used under the guidance of a healthcare professional.
